verdict.answer_first: B states the answer immediately: "Big-O describes how an algorithm's cost grows"; A also answers early but starts with a title and longer framing.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits cleanly to the key takeaway: "nested loop... reach for a hash map"; A is decisive too but adds more side paths and ends with "Want me to dig into..."
verdict.fabrication: A makes broader unsupported claims like "cover the majority" and "fib(50) will hang your machine"; B has mild broad claims but less inflated.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the same real traps in fewer steps: "'in' on my_list is O(n)... inside a loop... O(n²)".
